import io
import string
import list

fn parse_line(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  let tokens = string.split_whitespace(s)
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  mut i = 0
  while i < tokens.len()
    let tok = match tokens.get(i)
              when Some(t)
                t
              when None
                ""
              end
    match string.parse_int(tok)
    when Some(n)
      result = list.push(result, n)
    when None
      ()
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  result
end

fn get_at(_ xs: List<Int>, _ i: Int) -> Int
  match xs.get(i)
  when Some(v)
    v
  when None
    0
  end
end

fn main() -> Unit
  let line1 = match io.read_line()
              when Some(s)
                s
              when None
                ""
              end
  let line2 = match io.read_line()
              when Some(s)
                s
              when None
                ""
              end
  let a = parse_line(line1)
  let b = parse_line(line2)

  mut inter: List<Int> = []
  mut i = 0
  while i < a.len()
    let x = get_at(a, i)
    if list.contains(b, x) and not list.contains(inter, x)
      inter = list.push(inter, x)
    end
    i = i + 1
  end

  mut sorted: List<Int> = []
  mut j = 0
  while j < inter.len()
    let x = get_at(inter, j)
    mut new_sorted: List<Int> = []
    mut k = 0
    mut inserted = false
    while k < sorted.len()
      let y = get_at(sorted, k)
      if not inserted and x < y
        new_sorted = list.push(new_sorted, x)
        inserted = true
      end
      new_sorted = list.push(new_sorted, y)
      k = k + 1
    end
    if not inserted
      new_sorted = list.push(new_sorted, x)
    end
    sorted = new_sorted
    j = j + 1
  end

  if sorted.len() == 0
    println("empty")
  else
    mut out = ""
    mut m = 0
    while m < sorted.len()
      let v = get_at(sorted, m)
      if m == 0
        out = "#{v}"
      else
        out = "#{out} #{v}"
      end
      m = m + 1
    end
    println(out)
  end
end
